aloe vera.fun_fact: Ancient Egyptians called Aloe Vera the 'plant of immortality' and placed it among funeral gifts for pharaohs over 6,000 years ago.

faqs.q: Why are my Aloe leaves turning brown or soft?
faqs.a: Usually overwatering or root rot. Let the soil dry completely between waterings and use a well-draining succulent pot.

faqs.q: Can I use the gel directly on skin?
faqs.a: Yes! Cut a mature outer leaf, scoop the clear gel, and apply to burns or dry skin. Always patch-test first.

faqs.q: How much light does Aloe Vera need?
faqs.a: It thrives in bright, direct sunlight or very bright indirect light. Too little light causes weak, droopy leaves.

faqs.q: What kind of soil is best?
faqs.a: Use a well-drained cactus or succulent potting mix with extra perlite or sand to prevent moisture retention.

faqs.q: How do I propagate Aloe Vera?
faqs.a: Separate the offsets or 'pups' that grow at the base of the parent plant once they are a few inches tall.

monstera.fun_fact: Monstera's iconic split leaves evolved to let wind pass through without damaging the plant — and to allow sunlight to reach lower leaves in dense jungles.

faqs.q: Why doesn't my Monstera have holes yet?
faqs.a: Young leaves are solid. Splits (fenestrations) typically appear after 2-3 years if the plant receives sufficient bright indirect light.

faqs.q: Should I use a moss pole?
faqs.a: Yes! Monstera is a natural climber. A moss pole encourages larger leaves and a more compact, upright growth pattern.

faqs.q: Why are my Monstera leaves dripping water?
faqs.a: This is guttation. It happens when the soil is wet and the plant releases excess moisture. Reduce watering frequency.

faqs.q: What causes yellow leaves on a Monstera?
faqs.a: Typically overwatering or poor drainage. Check if soil is soggy, let it dry out, and ensure the pot has drainage holes.

faqs.q: How do I propagate my Monstera?
faqs.a: Cut just below a node (with or without aerial roots) and place the stem cutting in water or moist soil.

snake plant.fun_fact: NASA's Clean Air Study found that Snake Plants are one of the best air-purifying houseplants, converting CO₂ to oxygen even at night — making them perfect bedroom companions.

faqs.q: How often should I really water it?
faqs.a: Every 2-3 weeks in summer, once a month in winter. The #1 killer is overwatering. When in doubt, wait.

faqs.q: Can it survive in a dark room?
faqs.a: It tolerates low light but grows best in indirect sunlight. In very dark rooms, growth will be extremely slow.

faqs.q: Why is my Snake Plant falling over?
faqs.a: Usually root rot from overwatering or too deep a pot. Inspect roots, trim soft ones, and repot in dry, gritty soil.

faqs.q: How do I propagate a Snake Plant?
faqs.a: Divide the rhizomes during repotting, or cut a leaf into 3-inch sections and root them in water or sandy soil.

faqs.q: Why are the leaf tips turning brown?
faqs.a: Usually normal aging or minor physical damage. Trim the dead tips with clean shears, following the leaf shape.

pothos.fun_fact: In the wild, Pothos can climb trees up to 20 meters tall, and its leaves grow up to 100cm wide — nothing like the small-leaved houseplant version we know!

faqs.q: Why are the leaves turning yellow?
faqs.a: Usually overwatering. Check if the soil is soggy. Let the top inch dry out before watering again.

faqs.q: Can I grow it in just water?
faqs.a: Yes! Pothos thrives in water indefinitely. Change the water every 1-2 weeks and add liquid fertilizer monthly.

faqs.q: How do I make my Pothos bushier?
faqs.a: Prune the long vines. This stimulates new growth at the nodes and makes the plant fuller.

faqs.q: Why are the variegated patterns fading?
faqs.a: Too little light. Pothos needs bright, indirect light to maintain its white, cream, or yellow variegation.

faqs.q: Are Pothos toxic to pets?
faqs.a: Yes, they contain calcium oxalate crystals which cause mouth irritation and digestive discomfort. Keep them out of reach.

spider plant.fun_fact: Spider Plants are champion air purifiers and one of the easiest houseplants to propagate — those dangling 'babies' (plantlets) can be snipped and rooted in water within days.

faqs.q: Why do the leaf tips turn brown?
faqs.a: Typically caused by fluoride in tap water or low humidity. Try using filtered water and occasional misting.

faqs.q: How do I propagate the babies?
faqs.a: Snip a plantlet with a few inches of stem, place in water until roots form (~2 weeks), then plant in soil.

faqs.q: How much light does it need?
faqs.a: Bright, indirect light is ideal. They tolerate partial shade, but direct hot sun can scorch the leaves.

faqs.q: Why are the leaves pale or losing stripes?
faqs.a: Often a sign of too little light, under-fertilization, or the plant being severely root-bound.

faqs.q: How often should I water my Spider Plant?
faqs.a: Water thoroughly when the top 50% of the soil feels dry, usually once every 7-10 days.

peace lily.fun_fact: Peace Lilies are drama queens — they dramatically wilt when thirsty, but bounce back within hours of watering, making them the perfect plant for forgetful owners.

faqs.q: Why won't my Peace Lily flower?
faqs.a: It needs bright indirect light to bloom. In too-dark spots, it'll grow lush leaves but no white flowers.

faqs.q: Is it really toxic to pets?
faqs.a: Yes, it contains calcium oxalate crystals that cause mouth irritation if chewed. Keep away from curious cats and dogs.

faqs.q: Why are the leaves turning black or brown?
faqs.a: Usually chemical burn from tap water minerals, over-fertilizing, or direct sun scorch. Use filtered water.

faqs.q: How do I clean the leaves?
faqs.a: Wipe them down gently with a damp cloth every few weeks to keep them shiny and free of dust.

faqs.q: What type of soil does a Peace Lily prefer?
faqs.a: A well-draining potting mix rich in organic matter. Keep it consistently moist but not waterlogged.

rubber plant.fun_fact: Rubber Plants (Ficus elastica) were once tapped for their milky latex sap to produce natural rubber — before the rubber tree (Hevea brasiliensis) replaced them commercially.

faqs.q: Why is it dropping leaves?
faqs.a: Usually due to sudden temperature changes, cold drafts, or overwatering. Keep it in a stable, warm spot.

faqs.q: How do I make it branch out?
faqs.a: Prune the top growing point. This forces the plant to grow side shoots, creating a bushier shape.

faqs.q: Why are the leaves turning dull and dusty?
faqs.a: Wipe them with a damp cloth to remove dust. A clean leaf absorbs more light and stays healthy.

faqs.q: Is the white sap dangerous?
faqs.a: Yes, the latex sap is toxic and can cause skin irritation. Wear gloves when pruning or propagating.

faqs.q: How much sunlight does it require?
faqs.a: Bright, indirect light. Variegated cultivars need more light than the dark green varieties.

orchid.fun_fact: Orchids are the largest family of flowering plants with over 28,000 species — that's more than mammals, birds, and reptiles combined!

faqs.q: My orchid finished blooming. Is it dead?
faqs.a: No! Cut the flower spike above a node, keep watering weekly, and it can re-bloom in 2-3 months.

faqs.q: Should I use ice cubes to water?
faqs.a: It works but isn't ideal — orchids are tropical and prefer room-temperature water. Soak the roots for 10 minutes weekly.

faqs.q: Why are the roots turning gray or dry?
faqs.a: Healthy roots are green when wet and silvery-gray when dry. If gray, it's time to water.

faqs.q: What kind of potting medium do orchids need?
faqs.a: Never use standard soil. Orchids are epiphytes and need loose bark mix or sphagnum moss for aeration.

faqs.q: How do I trigger re-blooming?
faqs.a: Expose the orchid to slightly cooler night temperatures (around 15-18°C) for a few weeks in autumn.

zz plant.fun_fact: ZZ Plants store water in their thick potato-like rhizomes underground — they can survive months of neglect, earning the nickname 'the eternity plant.'

faqs.q: Is it really toxic?
faqs.a: It contains calcium oxalate crystals. Wash hands after handling and keep away from pets and children who might chew it.

faqs.q: Why is it growing so slowly?
faqs.a: ZZ Plants are naturally slow growers. In low light they grow even slower. Bright indirect light speeds things up.

faqs.q: Why are the stems bending or falling over?
faqs.a: This is usually due to severe overwatering causing stem/rhizome rot, or light deficiency causing leggy stems.

faqs.q: How often should I fertilize my ZZ Plant?
faqs.a: Only once or twice during the spring and summer growing season with a balanced liquid houseplant fertilizer.

faqs.q: How do I know if my ZZ Plant is overwatered?
faqs.a: Look for yellowing leaves, mushy brown stems, or a moldy smell from the soil.

calathea.fun_fact: Calatheas are called 'prayer plants' because they fold their leaves upward at night like hands in prayer — a process called nyctinasty driven by their internal clock.

faqs.q: Why are the leaf edges crispy?
faqs.a: Low humidity is the #1 cause. Calatheas love 50%+ humidity — use a humidifier or pebble tray.

faqs.q: Why are the colors fading?
faqs.a: Too much direct sun bleaches the patterns. Move to bright indirect light for the most vivid leaf colors.

faqs.q: What kind of water should I use?
faqs.a: Calatheas are highly sensitive to tap water chemicals. Use distilled, rainwater, or filtered water at room temperature.

faqs.q: How often should I water my Calathea?
faqs.a: Keep the soil consistently damp but never soggy. Water when the top inch of soil feels dry.

faqs.q: Why are there tiny webs on my Calathea?
faqs.a: A classic sign of spider mites, which love dry Calathea leaves. Increase humidity and treat with neem oil.

jade plant.fun_fact: In East Asian tradition, Jade Plants (Crassula ovata) are believed to attract wealth and prosperity — which is why they're a classic housewarming and business-opening gift.

faqs.q: Why are the leaves wrinkling?
faqs.a: Wrinkled leaves mean it's thirsty. Give it a thorough soak and it'll plump back up within a day.

faqs.q: Can it flower?
faqs.a: Yes! Mature Jade Plants (5+ years) can produce clusters of tiny star-shaped white-pink flowers in winter.

faqs.q: Why are the leaves falling off?
faqs.a: Often a result of overwatering, under-watering, or lack of light. Check soil moisture and adjust.

faqs.q: How do I prune a Jade plant?
faqs.a: Cut stems just above a leaf node. This encourages branching and helps maintain a sturdy, tree-like structure.

faqs.q: How much sun does it need?
faqs.a: At least 4-6 hours of direct sunlight daily. In low light, it will become leggy and weak.

boston fern.fun_fact: Boston Ferns are living fossils — ferns have existed for 360 million years, predating dinosaurs, flowers, and even seeds.

faqs.q: Why is it shedding so many leaves?
faqs.a: Low humidity or inconsistent watering. Ferns love moisture — mist daily and keep soil consistently damp (not soggy).

faqs.q: Can it go outside in summer?
faqs.a: Yes! They thrive on shaded porches. Just bring them in before temperatures drop below 10°C (50°F).

faqs.q: Why is my fern turning brown and dry?
faqs.a: Under-watering or dry air. Mist frequently, place in a bathroom, or use a pebble tray to boost humidity.

faqs.q: How do I fertilize my Boston Fern?
faqs.a: Feed monthly in spring and summer with a diluted, balanced liquid fertilizer. Avoid feeding in winter.

faqs.q: Can I trim dead fronds?
faqs.a: Yes! Cut dead or brown fronds at the base of the plant to keep it looking neat and encourage new growth.

dracaena.fun_fact: The Dragon Tree (Dracaena draco) gets its name from its blood-red resin called 'dragon's blood,' which was used as dye, medicine, and varnish for centuries.

faqs.q: Why are the lower leaves yellowing?
faqs.a: This is normal — Dracaenas naturally shed older lower leaves as they grow. Only worry if many leaves yellow at once.

faqs.q: Is fluoride in tap water a problem?
faqs.a: Yes! Dracaenas are very sensitive to fluoride, which causes brown leaf tips. Use filtered or distilled water.

faqs.q: How do I make my Dracaena bushier?
faqs.a: Cut the main stem. It will sprout new branches just below the cut, creating a fuller look.

faqs.q: What are the lighting requirements?
faqs.a: Medium to bright indirect light. Direct sun can burn leaves, while low light slows growth.

faqs.q: How often should I water my Dracaena?
faqs.a: Let the top 2 inches of soil dry completely between waterings. They are highly susceptible to root rot.

venus flytrap.fun_fact: Venus Flytraps can count! A trap only snaps shut after TWO touches within 20 seconds — this prevents wasting energy on raindrops and debris.

faqs.q: Should I feed it bugs?
faqs.a: Only if it's indoors and can't catch its own. One small insect per trap, once a month. Never feed it human food.

faqs.q: Why are the traps turning black?
faqs.a: Each trap has a lifespan of 3-5 closings, then dies. New traps grow from the center — it's normal.

faqs.q: What kind of water does it need?
faqs.a: Only use pure rainwater, distilled water, or demineralized water. Tap water will kill it due to minerals.

faqs.q: Does it need soil or fertilizer?
faqs.a: Never use standard soil or fertilizer. Use nutrient-poor peat moss and perlite. They get nutrients from prey.

faqs.q: Why is it dormant in winter?
faqs.a: They need a winter dormancy period of 3-4 months with cooler temperatures and less water to survive long-term.
